habitat: Brusy open subalpine areas; winters in foothills in scrubby areas; field edges; and gardens
story: The Blue Fronted Redstart (Phoenicurus frontalis) is an extraordinary bird that belongs to the Muscicapidae family. It is most commonly observed in Brusy open subalpine areas; winters in foothills in scrubby areas; field edges; and gardens, where its behaviors contribute to the ecological dynamics of the area. Listed as Least Concern (LC) by the IUCN, this species' future depends on continued conservation action. The story of the Blue Fronted Redstart is one of adaptation and resilience, offering valuable lessons for conservationists. This poster aims to bring the Blue Fronted Redstart into focus, highlighting its beauty and the importance of its habitat.
Every poster is crafted to inspire curiosity about our planet's incredible biodiversity.
